name: Crane, Simon, Clar & Goodman
description_short: Chicago-based law firm specializing in bankruptcy, reorganization, and commercial litigation for businesses and individuals since 1969.
description_long:
Crane, Simon, Clar & Goodman is an established law firm headquartered in Chicago that has served clients across Illinois, Wisconsin, Indiana, and the United States for over 40 years. The firm was founded in 1969 and operates as a three-partner firm with two of-counsel attorneys, collectively offering more than 200 years of combined experience in insolvency and commercial law.

The firm's primary services focus on bankruptcy representation across multiple chapters (Chapter 7, Chapter 11, and Chapter 13), as well as related services including business reorganization, restructuring, insolvency workouts, and commercial litigation. They represent business debtors of all sizes, individual debtors, secured and unsecured creditors, estate representatives, and creditors' committees. One of their attorneys serves as a Chapter 7 trustee appointed by the United States Trustee's Office for the Northern District of Illinois.

What distinguishes this firm is their explicit commitment to exploring alternatives beyond bankruptcy filing. They position themselves as "financial problem-solvers" who evaluate workouts, negotiations, assignments for the benefit of creditors, and litigation alongside bankruptcy options. They specifically target small to midsize businesses, offering the depth and breadth of experience of larger firms while maintaining the cost-effectiveness and personalized attention typical of smaller operations. Attorneys regularly write and lecture on bankruptcy and reorganization topics.

This is a legitimate legal services firm, not a debt settlement or credit repair company. The main caveat is that as attorneys, they require formal engagement and will charge legal fees. They are most appropriate for clients who need professional legal representation in bankruptcy court or related insolvency matters, not for those seeking quick debt relief or credit repair services.
pros: Over 40 years of continuous operation since 1969 with established reputation
Collective 200+ years of attorney experience specifically in bankruptcy and insolvency law
One attorney serves as appointed Chapter 7 trustee for Northern District of Illinois
Explicitly evaluates bankruptcy alternatives (workouts, negotiations, assignments for benefit of creditors)
Focuses on small to midsize businesses, avoiding prohibitive large-firm fees
Attorneys are frequent writers and lecturers on bankruptcy and reorganization
Located in Chicago's financial district (LaSalle Street) with multi-state service area
cons: Attorney-based legal services with associated professional fees, not a budget solution
Primary service is formal bankruptcy filing and litigation, not debt settlement or negotiation-first approaches
Limited information on website about specific fee structures or cost transparency
Not appropriate for consumers seeking quick credit repair or non-legal debt solutions
Requires formal legal engagement process rather than immediate consumer-facing services
best_for: Small to midsize business owners considering Chapter 7, 11, or 13 bankruptcy protection
Individual debtors with complex financial situations requiring legal representation in bankruptcy court
Creditors and creditors' committees seeking representation in bankruptcy proceedings
Business owners exploring restructuring and insolvency alternatives before or instead of bankruptcy
diagnosis: Best for business owners and individual debtors who need professional legal representation in bankruptcy court or require guidance on restructuring and insolvency alternatives. Main caveat: this is a law firm charging professional fees, not a consumer-facing debt relief service, and requires formal legal engagement rather than offering immediate or low-cost solutions.
